model.label: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview
model.description: Google • Preview Gemini model tuned for high-end multimodal reasoning and long-context analysis.

pick.rationale: Michigan enters this Sweet 16 matchup as a dominant force, boasting top-two rankings in both KenPom and Torvik with an elite top-10 offense and the number two defense nationally. While Alabama features a potent top-three scoring attack, their 67th-ranked defense leaves them highly vulnerable against the Wolverines' balanced execution. Furthermore, the Crimson Tide's offensive ceiling is severely compromised by the absence of second-leading scorer Aden Holloway, who is unavailable for the tournament. With a massive 13-point advantage in adjusted efficiency margin and a suffocating defensive unit, Michigan is the highly confident pick to advance to the Elite Eight.

reasoningStep.title: Analyze Michigan vs Alabama Matchup
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan's elite defense and overall efficiency margin give them a significant edge over an Alabama team missing a key contributor.
evidence: Michigan ranks 2nd in both KenPom and Torvik, featuring the #2 adjusted defense and a top-10 offense.
Alabama ranks 16th in KenPom with the #3 offense but a vulnerable 67th-ranked defense.
Alabama is missing second-leading scorer Aden Holloway due to an off-court issue, significantly lowering their offensive ceiling.
Michigan star Yaxel Lendeborg is playing through a minor ankle sprain, but the Wolverines' massive 13-point advantage in adjusted efficiency margin makes them the clear favorite.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
